name: discord-automation
description: Automate Discord tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): messages, channels, roles, webhooks, reactions. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Send and edit messages in Discord channels via bot
Create and manage direct messages to Discord users
Create, assign, and delete roles in Discord servers
Create and manage webhooks for external integrations
List guilds, channels, and members for automation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) that provides guidance on using Rube MCP for Discord automation. All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown formatting for tool names, the URL is a legitimate service endpoint, and there is no executable code or malicious content. The skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Manual Discord management takes time and is prone to errors. This skill enables Claude to automate Discord operations including messaging, role management, webhooks, and reactions through Rube MCP integration.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection to function
Bot must have appropriate Discord permissions (SEND_MESSAGES, MANAGE_ROLES, etc.)
Rate limits apply to prevent Discord API throttling
Cannot edit messages sent by other bots or users

useCases.title: Community Manager Notifications
useCases.description: Automate welcome messages, announcements, and moderation alerts in Discord servers

useCases.title: DevOps Deployment Alerts
useCases.description: Set up automated notifications for CI/CD pipelines, server status, and deployment events

useCases.title: Bot Development Prototyping
useCases.description: Quickly test and prototype Discord bot functionality before building a full bot

promptTemplates.title: Send a Welcome Message
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to send a welcome message to channel ID {channel_id} saying 'Welcome to our server!'
promptTemplates.scenario: When a new member joins the Discord server

promptTemplates.title: Create and Assign a Role
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to create a new role called 'Verified' in guild {guild_id} and assign it to user {user_id}

promptTemplates.title: Set Up Webhook for Alerts
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to create a webhook in channel {channel_id} named 'DeployAlerts' and show me how to execute it
promptTemplates.scenario: When setting up external service notifications

promptTemplates.title: Manage Server Reactions
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the discord-automation skill to list all users who reacted with '✅' to message {message_id} in channel {channel_id}
promptTemplates.scenario: When tracking event RSVPs or approvals

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before starting a workflow
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Discord operations
Respect Discord rate limits and handle 429 responses with Retry-After headers
antiPatterns: Assuming the bot has permissions it does not have - always verify permission setup first
Ignoring rate limits which can lead to temporary API bans
Using user operations (discord toolkit) instead of bot operations (discordbot) for automation

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a service by Composio that provides Model Context Protocol access to various integrations including Discord. It acts as a bridge between Claude and Discord's API.

faq.question: Do I need API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. Rube MCP does not require API keys. You only need to add the MCP server endpoint and complete Discord OAuth authentication.

faq.question: Can this skill send messages as me or as a bot?
faq.answer: The skill supports both. Use the discordbot toolkit for bot token operations or the discord toolkit for user OAuth operations. Bot operations are preferred for automation.

faq.question: What permissions does the bot need?
faq.answer: Permissions vary by operation: SEND_MESSAGES for messaging, MANAGE_ROLES for role operations, MANAGE_WEBHOOKS for webhook management, and MANAGE_MESSAGES for reaction cleanup.

faq.question: How do I get channel and user IDs?
faq.answer: Enable Developer Mode in Discord settings, then right-click on any channel, user, or message to copy its ID. IDs are 64-bit snowflake numbers.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in production?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides documentation for using official Discord APIs. However, always test workflows in a development server first and follow Discord's rate limit guidelines.
